## Structure

TCP S→C 0x8381 — `AuthAck`. Server's response to the client's
`Auth` (0x8480) request. Fixed 31-byte body across all 50
retail observations from 17/17 captures.

```
[0..1]   83 81                  TCP opcode (constant)
[2..5]   account_id LE32        the account's numeric id
[6..9]   reserved LE32          CONSTANT 0x00000000
[10..11] session_len LE16       CONSTANT 0x0012 (=18)
[12..29] session_data           18-byte server-generated session
                                nonce (varies per attempt; appears
                                to be a cipher/session nonce)
[30]     trailer u8             varies: 0x00 dominant, 0x2d also
                                observed in retail
```

Sample retail bytes:
```
8381 bd7e0100  00000000  1200  c29999f9b1b959e973a94b39b7054e9944  59 2d
   account_id=0x00017ebd HANNIBAL/AUGUSTO; trailer=0x2d
8381 b0a20000  00000000  1200  8e3b509b70eb107b36fb024b72f70e7b09  db 2d
   account_id=0x0000a2b0 PLAZA; trailer=0x2d
8381 bd7e0100  00000000  1200  f848cb88e6188358ad087828ef5474c87c  98 00
   account_id=0x00017ebd; trailer=0x00
```

The 18-byte `session_data` is byte-wise distinct across all
retail samples — even for the same account it varies per
session.

## Variants

Single 31-byte form. The `session_data` content is the only
varying field (random/server-generated). The trailer byte at
[30] takes either 0x00 or 0x2d in retail; cause of the
distinction is unclear (no clear time/account correlation).

## Observed contexts

Emitted right after the client's `Auth` (0x8480) request as
the gameserver's success acknowledgement. Top marker `RESUME`
correlates with the resume-login flow.

50 observations / 17 captures. Each session sees this 2-6
times — likely once per login (initial + reconnect).

The infoserver flavor (`server.infoserver.packets.server_tcp.AuthAck`)
shares the same 0x8381 opcode but is a different listener.

## Open questions

- What determines trailer byte 0x00 vs 0x2d? Possibly a
  server-side flag (account-tier, login source, or zone
  affinity). Not byte-stable per account or per session.
- The 18-byte `session_data` is varied per attempt — is it a
  fresh nonce that the client uses for subsequent
  encryption / session-binding? Ceres-J emits 18 zero bytes
  and the client still works (length check satisfied), so
  semantics are likely opaque to the client (server-side
  reference token).

## Server-side handler

`server.gameserver.packets.server_tcp.AuthAck` —
emits the verified 31-byte body. Account ID is dynamic; the
18-byte `session_data` is currently zero-padded (which is
sufficient for client state transition per source-code
comment).

Companion: `server.infoserver.packets.server_tcp.AuthAck` —
same wire shape, different listener.

Wired from:
- `server.gameserver.packets.client_tcp.Auth.java:68` —
  emitted on successful auth.
- `server.infoserver.packets.client_tcp.Auth.java:30` — same.

Tests pin the 31-byte byte identity in the Parity audit (task
#126).
